title: Bug: storage read_path_bytes uses stringly-typed errors; route layer falls back to string-matching
description:
## Summary

`JsonFileStorage::read_path_bytes` (crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:752) erases a real `io::ErrorKind::NotFound` into `anyhow!(\"File not found: {}\", path)` — a string-tagged anyhow error. The server routes then reconstruct HTTP status by doing `e.to_string().contains(\"not found\")` (`crates/server/src/routes.rs:350-356` and sibling handlers). This is brittle error classification; a refactor of either the error string or a new error message elsewhere could silently change HTTP status codes.

CLAUDE.md states: "Result-based errors — thiserror custom types with descriptive messages. No panics in library code." The rest of the project uses typed errors; this code path is an outlier.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A typed error enum (e.g. `PathReadError` in `crates/jit/src/storage/` or a dedicated `errors` submodule) exists with at least `NotFound(String)` and a catch-all `Other(anyhow::Error)` (or equivalent).
- [ ] `IssueStore::read_path_bytes` trait signature returns `Result<(Vec<u8>, String), PathReadError>` (or the chosen error type).
- [ ] Both `JsonFileStorage` and `InMemoryStorage` return `PathReadError::NotFound` for missing files (for both git-commit and working-tree branches, as appropriate).
- [ ] Route handlers in `crates/server/src/routes.rs` that currently use `e.to_string().contains(\"not found\")` for path-based document endpoints are replaced with typed `match`/`if let` dispatch on the new error variant. No string-matching remains in the document-raw / document-content / document-by-path paths.
- [ ] Route handlers still return HTTP 404 for NotFound and 500 for Other errors — covered by tests for each code path.
- [ ] No new clippy warnings. All tests pass.
- [ ] cargo-ci + code-review gates both pass.

## Scope

Limit to: the `read_path_bytes` trait and its direct call sites. Do NOT refactor the broader anyhow-based error handling in the rest of the codebase — that's separate technical debt tracked elsewhere.

## Context

Surfaced during task 42bd50ce's code-review gate re-run after wave-1 work. Reviewer's finding has been flagged in three consecutive rounds in different phrasings but same root cause. User explicitly approved fixing this before closing wave 1 (per escalation rule 5a).
